meta.role: Graduate Programs Student Services Support Coordinator

summary: I'm Jamie-a curious People & Organizational Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact.

bullets.text: Conduct focus groups and 1:1 interviews with an NGO leadership team to surface decision-making and accountability gaps
Upskill HR team in data analytics to audit 300+ leave cases, refuting cost assumptions via evidence-based insights
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational programs
Develop guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, improving program knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%
Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining program positioning, delivery strategy, and operations via feedback analysis
Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, enhancing program understanding and driving a 78% program enrollment rate
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming feedback data into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Optimized onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing cross-functional stakeholders
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology
